title: Use VISU_MANCONTROL Screen Manual Controls For Tipper Functions
summary: Use the Operator Station HMI VISU_MANCONTROL screen, as seen by personnel logged in as maintenance, to perform documented manual control actions for the corresponding tipper. The source documents GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE, LAMP TEST, LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET, and LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE, and states that AIR BLOWOFF is not currently used.
when_to_use: Use when maintenance-level personnel need to access the VISU_MANCONTROL screen and perform one of the documented manual control actions for a tipper: manually open or close the gripper, run a lamp test, reset a tipper after a fault, or disable a tipper.
not_for: Do not use AIR BLOWOFF from this screen because the source states this button is not currently used.
Do not use this runbook as a complete safety or recovery procedure; the source does not provide safety prerequisites, interlocks, or recovery detail for these manual controls.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Log in with maintenance-level access, navigate to VISU_MANCONTROL, select the documented manual control, and verify the documented effect.

safety_notes: This runbook is not support-safe based on the source packet; the controls can directly affect tipper behavior.
The source does not provide prerequisites, constraints, interlocks, or safe-use boundaries for manual actuation on this screen.
Review by an SME is needed before exposing this procedure for routine support use.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual and audible access to the corresponding stacklight and alarm

access_required: Maintenance-level login on the Operator Station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_MANCONTROL screen is accessible on the Operator Station HMI
The user can observe the corresponding stacklight and hear the audible alarm when verifying LAMP TEST or TIPPER DISABLE results

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI Manual Control Screen for maintenance users showing GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE, LAMP TEST, LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET, LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE, AIR BLOWOFF, and related status/navigation elements.
Operator station display controls showing screen navigation, including F1 mapped to Visu_ManControl.
Operator Station HMI screen showing login/logout controls and logged-in user display layout relevant to confirming HMI access context.

steps.title: Log in and open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen
steps.purpose: Access the maintenance-only manual control screen that contains the documented tipper manual controls.
steps.instruction: Log in on the Operator Station HMI with maintenance-level access and open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen is displayed for a maintenance-level user.
steps.healthy_condition: The manual control screen is visible and corresponds to the maintenance-user view documented in Figure 4-23.
steps.failure_condition: Maintenance-level access is not available or the VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be opened.

actions.label: Log in as maintenance
actions.exact_action: Log in with maintenance-level access on the Operator Station HMI.

actions.label: Open VISU_MANCONTROL
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The maintenance-user VISU_MANCONTROL screen layout and manual control buttons.
Screen navigation controls showing F1 mapped to Visu_ManControl.

stop_or_escalate_if: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen cannot be reached from the Operator Station HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen shown below is what personnel logged in as maintenance will see.
The listed control mapping includes F1 to "Visu_ManControl."

steps.title: Identify the required manual control
steps.purpose: Select the documented control that matches the intended tipper action.
steps.instruction: On the VISU_MANCONTROL screen, identify the corresponding manual control for the intended action: GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE, LAMP TEST, LEFT TIPPER FAULT RESET, RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET, LEFT TIPPER DISABLE, or RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE.
steps.expected_result: The correct manual control for the intended tipper action is identified before actuation.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected control matches one of the functions explicitly documented in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The intended action does not match a documented control on the screen or the operator is uncertain which control applies.

actions.label: Review available controls
actions.exact_action: Identify the corresponding manual control for the intended tipper action on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The manual control buttons for GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE, LAMP TEST, LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET, LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE, and AIR BLOWOFF.

stop_or_escalate_if: The intended action is not one of the documented VISU_MANCONTROL functions.
There is uncertainty about which tipper control should be used.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE manually opens or closes the gripper; LAMP TEST sounds the audible alarm and flashes the stacklight red; LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET resets the corresponding tipper after a fault; LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE disables the corresponding tipper and illuminates the stacklight solid blue.

steps.title: Use GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE if manual gripper actuation is needed
steps.purpose: Manually open or close the gripper for the corresponding tipper using the documented HMI control.
steps.instruction: If the intended action is gripper actuation, press GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE for the corresponding tipper to manually open or close the gripper.
steps.expected_result: The corresponding tipper gripper manually opens or closes.
steps.healthy_condition: The gripper changes state as commanded by the GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE control.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper does not open or close after the control is pressed.

actions.label: Press GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE
actions.exact_action: Press the GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE button for the corresponding tipper to manually open or close the gripper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE control on the maintenance manual control screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gripper does not manually open or close after pressing GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE manually opens or closes the gripper.

steps.title: Use LAMP TEST and verify alarm indications
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented audible and visual alarm indications using the lamp test control.
steps.instruction: If the intended action is a lamp test, press LAMP TEST and verify that the audible alarm sounds and the stacklight flashes red.
steps.expected_result: The audible alarm sounds and the stacklight flashes red.
steps.healthy_condition: Both the audible alarm and flashing red stacklight occur after pressing LAMP TEST.
steps.failure_condition: The audible alarm does not sound, the stacklight does not flash red, or only part of the documented indication occurs.

actions.label: Verify audible and visual response
actions.exact_action: Verify that the audible alarm sounds and the stacklight flashes red.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The LAMP TEST control on the maintenance manual control screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The audible alarm does not sound after pressing LAMP TEST.
The stacklight does not flash red after pressing LAMP TEST.
The documented effect does not occur.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: LAMP TEST sounds the audible alarm and flashes the stacklight red.

steps.title: Use LEFT or RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET after a fault
steps.purpose: Reset the corresponding tipper after a fault using the documented manual control.
steps.instruction: If the intended action is fault reset, press LEFT TIPPER FAULT RESET or RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET for the corresponding tipper after a fault.
steps.expected_result: The corresponding tipper is reset after a fault.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected tipper fault reset control is accepted and the tipper is reset after the fault condition.
steps.failure_condition: The corresponding tipper does not reset after pressing the fault reset control.

actions.label: Press tipper fault reset
actions.exact_action: Press the fault reset button for the corresponding tipper to reset it after a fault.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET controls on the maintenance manual control screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The corresponding tipper does not reset after pressing the fault reset control.
The source does not provide additional recovery detail for the fault condition.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET resets the corresponding tipper after a fault.

steps.title: Use LEFT or RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE and verify blue stacklight
steps.purpose: Disable the corresponding tipper using the documented manual control and verify the documented indication.
steps.instruction: If the intended action is tipper disable, press LEFT TIPPER DISABLE or RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE for the corresponding tipper and verify that the stacklight illuminates solid blue.
steps.expected_result: The corresponding tipper is disabled and the stacklight illuminates solid blue.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected tipper is disabled and the stacklight is solid blue.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper does not disable or the stacklight does not illuminate solid blue.

actions.label: Press tipper disable
actions.exact_action: Press the disable button for the corresponding tipper.

actions.label: Verify blue stacklight
actions.exact_action: Verify that the stacklight illuminates solid blue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE controls on the maintenance manual control screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The corresponding tipper does not disable after pressing the disable control.
The stacklight does not illuminate solid blue.
The documented effect does not occur.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE disables the corresponding tipper and illuminates the stacklight solid blue.

steps.title: Do not use AIR BLOWOFF from this screen
steps.purpose: Prevent use of a control that the source explicitly states is not currently used.
steps.instruction: Do not use the AIR BLOWOFF button on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.
steps.expected_result: AIR BLOWOFF is not actuated from this procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The AIR BLOWOFF button is left unused.
steps.failure_condition: AIR BLOWOFF is pressed despite the source stating it is not currently used.

actions.label: Avoid AIR BLOWOFF
actions.exact_action: Do not use AIR BLOWOFF because the source states that this button is not currently used.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The AIR BLOWOFF button on the maintenance manual control screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is a request to use AIR BLOWOFF from this screen despite the source stating it is not currently used.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: AIR BLOWOFF is not currently used.

success_criteria: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen is accessed with maintenance-level login.
The intended documented control is selected and actuated.

The selected control produces the documented effect: gripper opens/closes, lamp test sounds the audible alarm and flashes the stacklight red, fault reset resets the corresponding tipper after a fault, or tipper disable illuminates the stacklight solid blue.
healthy_conditions: The selected manual control matches a function documented in Figure 4-23 and Table 4-9.
Observed machine or indication response matches the source-described effect.
failure_conditions: The selected control does not produce the documented effect.
The audible alarm or stacklight behavior does not match the documented lamp test or disable indication.
AIR BLOWOFF is considered for use even though the source states it is not currently used.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if maintenance-level access is not available.
Escalate if the documented effect does not occur after pressing the selected control.
Escalate for SME review because the source does not provide safety prerequisites, interlocks, or recovery detail for these manual controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall VISU_MANCONTROL maintenance-user screen and manual control buttons.
Navigation controls showing access to Visu_ManControl.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The screen shown below is what personnel logged in as maintenance will see. GRIPPERS OPEN/CLOSE manually opens or closes the gripper; LAMP TEST sounds the audible alarm and flashes the stacklight red; LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER FAULT RESET resets the corresponding tipper after a fault; LEFT/RIGHT TIPPER DISABLE disables the corresponding tipper and illuminates the stacklight solid blue; AIR BLOWOFF is not currently used.

missing_details: The source does not provide explicit safety prerequisites or interlocks for using these manual controls.
The source does not state whether production must be stopped before using these controls.
The source does not state whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source does not provide estimated execution time.
The source does not provide detailed recovery actions if a control does not produce the documented effect.
The source does not describe confirmation details beyond the documented effects for gripper open/close and fault reset.
